name: Granny's Tacos
recipeIngredient: 1 medium Potato, peeled, boiled, and mashed (or use leftover)
recipeInstructions:
1. Let the tortillas reach room temperature before using.
2. Use a well-seasoned iron skillet for frying.
3. Filling Preparation:
a. Crumble hamburger into the skillet.
b. Add onion and garlic.
c. Cook until the meat is browned and onions are nearly translucent.
d. Incorporate mashed potatoes, stirring gently.
e. Season with garlic salt and pepper to taste.
f. Keep the mixture hot and ensure potatoes are well-incorporated.
4. To Fry:
a. Heat oil in the skillet to a depth of about 1/16 to 1/8 inch.
b. Add more oil as needed during cooking.
c. Cook at least two tacos simultaneously in an average-sized skillet.
d. Ensure they don't burn; a drop of water should sizzle when added.
e. Place a tortilla stack on tongs, add a spoonful of filling in the center.
f. Fold into a taco shape and gently place it in the hot oil to set.
g. Flip using tongs to fry the other side until crispy.
h. Remove from the skillet with paper towels to drain excess oil.
5. Assembling:
a. Add cheese first.
b. Toss in desired liquid condiments (salsa, guacamole, etc.).
c. Place small chunks of ingredients.
d. Finish with shredded lettuce.
6. Serve and enjoy the tacos. They may be messy but are delicious!
